text:
(a) On receipt of a report under Section 522.104 (Submission of Report to Department) , the department shall disqualify the person from driving a commercial motor vehicle under Section 522.081 (Disqualification) beginning on the 45th day after the date the report is received unless a hearing is granted.

(b) Except as provided by Subsection (c), the procedure for notice and disqualification under this section is that specified by Subchapters C (Suspension Effective Date) and D, Chapter 724 (Implied Consent) , or Chapter 524 (Administrative Suspension of Driver’s License for Failure to Pass Test for Intoxication) .

(c) The department shall disqualify the person from driving a commercial motor vehicle for the period authorized by this chapter if, in a hearing held under this section, the court finds that:

(1) probable cause existed that the person was driving a commercial motor vehicle while having alcohol, a controlled substance, or a drug in the person’s system;

(2) the person was offered an opportunity to give a specimen under this chapter; and

(3) the person submitted a specimen that disclosed an alcohol concentration of 0.04 or more or refused to submit a specimen.

(d) An appeal of a disqualification under this section is subject to Sections 524.041-524.044.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 522. Commercial Driver's Licenses
##### § 522.105 — Disqualification of Driver

(a) On receipt of a report under Section 522.104 (Submission of Report to Department) , the department shall disqualify the person from driving a commercial motor vehicle under Section 522.081 (Disqualification) beginning on the 45th day after the date the report is received unless a hearing is granted.

(b) Except as provided by Subsection (c), the procedure for notice and disqualification under this section is that specified by Subchapters C (Suspension Effective Date) and D, Chapter 724 (Implied Consent) , or Chapter 524 (Administrative Suspension of Driver’s License for Failure to Pass Test for Intoxication) .

(c) The department shall disqualify the person from driving a commercial motor vehicle for the period authorized by this chapter if, in a hearing held under this section, the court finds that:

    (1) probable cause existed that the person was driving a commercial motor vehicle while having alcohol, a controlled substance, or a drug in the person’s system;

    (2) the person was offered an opportunity to give a specimen under this chapter; and

    (3) the person submitted a specimen that disclosed an alcohol concentration of 0.04 or more or refused to submit a specimen.

(d) An appeal of a disqualification under this section is subject to Sections 524.041-524.044.

*Acts 2015, 84th Leg., R.S., Ch. 752 (H.B.1888), Sec. 36, eff. January 1, 2016.*

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: Except as provided by Subsection (c), the procedure for notice and disqualification under this section is that specified by Subchapters C (Suspension Effective Date) and D, Chapter 724 (Implied Consent) , or Chapter 524 (Administrative Suspension of Driver’s License for Failure to Pass Test for Intoxication) .

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: The department shall disqualify the person from driving a commercial motor vehicle for the period authorized by this chapter if, in a hearing held under this section, the court finds that:
probable cause existed that the person was driving a commercial motor vehicle while having alcohol, a controlled substance, or a drug in the person’s system;
the person was offered an opportunity to give a specimen under this chapter; and
the person submitted a specimen that disclosed an alcohol concentration of 0.04 or more or refused to submit a specimen.

subsections.label: d
subsections.text: An appeal of a disqualification under this section is subject to Sections 524.041-524.044.

history.raw: Acts 2015, 84th Leg., R.S., Ch. 752 (H.B.1888), Sec. 36, eff. January 1, 2016.
